reason: The core claims are well-supported. The Christmas garden scene (Scrimgeour's recruitment attempt at the Burrow) is confirmed cut from HBP film - Scrimgeour was entirely absent from that film despite Bill Nighy being originally cast for it. The HP Wiki explicitly notes 'his relationship with Harry is far from antagonistic' in the film and 'his negative personality aspects never come to light in the way and to the extent they do in the book' and 'he was much more civil and patient' in DH1. The narrative_role and motivations deductions are strongly supported. The personality score is also directionally correct. The one mildly questionable claim is the 'haunted/near-resignation' beat in the film's closing scene - this is a subjective read of Bill Nighy's performance that isn't independently corroborated, but it's not factually wrong either. A score of 60/100 is reasonable given Scrimgeour appears in only one film (DH1) in a reduced role, with his entire HBP arc cut, though some might argue it should be slightly lower given how much was lost.
